commands.author: simplyclever914

target.title: Add reset family carryover summaries
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Depends on #90239.

When a session has been reset/rotated, the active transcript can be clean while the previous reset archive still contains the latest compaction summary. The next embedded-agent prompt therefore starts without the carryover context unless the model/tool explicitly asks for family history.

This change reuses the session-family transcript resolver from #90239 and injects the latest reset-archive compaction summary into embedded-agent prompt context before the LLM boundary, but only when the active context does not already contain a compaction summary.

## Fix

- Move the #90239 family/reset transcript target resolver into a shared gateway helper.
- Keep `chat.history(includeFamily)` on the shared resolver.
- Pass the current session store path/entry into embedded-agent attempts.
- Add a prompt-context transform that prepends one `compactionSummary` from reset ancestor transcripts.
- Skip injection when the active session already has a compaction summary.
- Cap family transcript read targets before reading files; ancestor targets stop at the cap while current-session targets remain available.
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserved the newer transcript-reader seam plus same-session reset fallback.

## Real behavior proof

**Current head proven:** `ba74ecd54e52c2416ab8f7906491266b989f529c`.

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A reset/rotated logical session should read bounded reset-family history through the shared resolver, keep current reset archive plus current active transcript available inside the cap, preserve the newest eligible ancestors even when an older retained ancestor has many reset-topic archives, and inject exactly one reset-family compaction summary before the embedded-agent LLM boundary.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `/home/clever/.openclaw/workspace/github/openclaw`, using current branch TypeScript modules and temporary on-disk session stores/transcripts under `/tmp/openclaw-family-proof-ba74ecd54e-*`. No private channel, endpoint, phone, prompt, auth, or user transcript data was used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
ba74ecd54e52c2416ab8f7906491266b989f529c

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# The script imported these current-branch modules:
# - ./src/gateway/session-history-family.ts
# - ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# It created a temporary sessions.json, 35 ancestor transcripts,
# 35 owned reset-topic archives for ancestor 05, one current reset archive,
# and one current active transcript. It called the real family target resolver,
# real carryover resolver, installed the prompt transform, applied the transform
# twice, printed the observed result, and removed temp files.
TS
{
  "head": "ba74ecd54e52c2416ab8f7906491266b989f529c",
  "targetCount": 32,
  "firstTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-05:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-05.jsonl",
    "ancestor-proof-06:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-06.jsonl",
    "ancestor-proof-07:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-07.jsonl",
    "ancestor-proof-08:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-08.jsonl"
  ],
  "lastTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-33:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-33.jsonl",
    "ancestor-proof-34:ancestor-active:ancestor-proof-34.jsonl",
    "current-proof-session:reset-archive:current-proof-session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-04T00-04-00.000Z",
    "current-proof-session:active:current-proof-session.jsonl"
  ],
  "currentResetTargetPresent": true,
  "currentActiveLast": true,
  "ancestor04Present": false,
  "ancestor34Present": true,
  "archiveHeavyTopicTargetCount": 0,
  "carryoverSummary": "Exact-head ba74ecd54e carryover 34",
  "carryoverTokensBefore": 2034,
  "promptRolesBeforeLlm": ["compactionSummary", "user"],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1,
  "idempotentRoles": ["compactionSummary", "user"]
}
```

**Evidence after fix:**

- The shared resolver returned 32 targets and kept both current reset archive and current active transcript inside the cap.
- The current active transcript was ordered last for final history trimming.
- The old over-cap ancestor was not read: `ancestor04Present` is false.
- The newest eligible ancestor survived even though older retained ancestor 05 had 35 owned reset-topic archives: `ancestor34Present` is true and `archiveHeavyTopicTargetCount` is 0.
- Carryover selected the newest retained ancestor summary, `Exact-head ba74ecd54e carryover 34`, and preserved its token count.
- The embedded prompt transform injected exactly one `compactionSummary` before the model boundary and remained idempotent on a second pass.

**Validation on current head:**

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'family|includeFamily|cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed | 39 skipped (43)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'family|includeFamily|cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 18 skipped (22)

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
# passed; public package SDK entrypoints: exports 10327, callable exports 5184, deprecated exports 3247

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  5 passed (5)

$ CI=true pnpm check:test-types
# passed

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

**What was not tested:** Full live Telegram/Slack/Discord end-to-end reset turn was not run locally; the proof exercises the real resolver and embedded carryover path against on-disk OpenClaw-like session artifacts.
## Current head addendum: `f59176b8ac`

ClawSweeper's exact-head proof request is addressed on current head `f59176b8acbb9847ab4c69c09ad4539cde66b9d0`.

The carryover prompt injection is now default-off and requires explicit `agents.defaults.experimental.sessionFamilyCarryover: true`. The proof below uses current-branch modules, temporary on-disk reset-family transcript files, the real reset-family carryover resolver, and the real transform installer/gate.

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
f59176b8acbb9847ab4c69c09ad4539cde66b9d0

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Creates a temporary ancestor reset archive with a compaction row and a current active transcript.
# Imports current-branch:
# - ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# Resolves carryover from reset-family transcript files, checks default-off/raw/opt-in gate behavior,
# installs the transform only for explicit opt-in, applies it before the model boundary, prints result,
# and removes temp files.
TS
{
  "head": "f59176b8acbb9847ab4c69c09ad4539cde66b9d0",
  "defaultOffInstalls": false,
  "rawOptInInstalls": false,
  "explicitOptInInstalls": true,
  "carryoverSummary": "Exact-head proof archived carryover summary.",
  "carryoverTokensBefore": 1234,
  "defaultOffRolesBeforeLlm": [
    "user"
  ],
  "explicitOptInRolesBeforeLlm": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1
}
```

Observed result after fix:

- default config does not install reset-family carryover and leaves model-bound roles as `user` only
- raw model runs do not install reset-family carryover even when the experimental opt-in is true
- explicit `sessionFamilyCarryover` opt-in installs the transform and prepends exactly one archived reset-family compaction summary before the user message
- the carryover resolver read the summary from the reset-family transcript archive and preserved `tokensBefore`

Validation after the stacked #90239 CI repair cherry-pick:

```text
CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -t 'fallback behavior with provider cooldowns'
# 2 files / 14 selected tests passed

CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
# 2 files / 21 tests passed

CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
# 2 files / 28 tests passed

CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

git diff --check
# no output
```
## Current head addendum: `1f2e39b6d9`

ClawSweeper's current-head proof request is refreshed on head `1f2e39b6d9f93c541c1d15549620b9e4a27fd751` after the selector/offset validation and embedded/Gateway family-history parity updates.

The carryover prompt-boundary proof below uses current-branch TypeScript modules directly and no private transcript data:

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
1f2e39b6d9f93c541c1d15549620b9e4a27fd751

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Imports current-branch ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# Checks default-off, raw-run, explicit opt-in, transform insertion, and idempotence.
TS
{
  "head": "1f2e39b6d9f93c541c1d15549620b9e4a27fd751",
  "defaultOffInstalls": false,
  "rawOptInInstalls": false,
  "explicitOptInInstalls": true,
  "rolesBeforeLlm": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1,
  "idempotentRoles": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ]
}
```

Additional exact-head validation after the embedded family-history parity fix and includeFamily/offset validation:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'family|includeFamily|subagent_announce|sessionStartedAt|offset'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  22 passed | 18 skipped (40)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'family|includeFamily|offset|cap'
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  16 passed | 107 skipped (123)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Observed result after fix:

- `includeFamily` remains the only selector and `includeFamily + offset` is rejected in tool, Gateway, and embedded paths.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now skips the final current-session `sessionStartedAt` announce filter, matching Gateway, so ancestor/reset `subagent_announce` rows are preserved in family history.
- Reset-family carryover remains default-off, skips raw model runs, and injects exactly one archived compaction summary only under explicit opt-in.
## Current head addendum: `1ca3a90b24`

Follow-up head `1ca3a90b24bed1bf8aa0a855614afa8d5f70d45d` keeps the same behavior as the `1f2e39b6d9` proof and only adjusts the new embedded regression test to satisfy lint and test-type gates.

Runtime carryover proof on the exact current head:

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
1ca3a90b24bed1bf8aa0a855614afa8d5f70d45d

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Imports current-branch ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# Checks default-off, raw-run, explicit opt-in, transform insertion, and idempotence.
TS
{
  "head": "1ca3a90b24bed1bf8aa0a855614afa8d5f70d45d",
  "defaultOffInstalls": false,
  "rawOptInInstalls": false,
  "explicitOptInInstalls": true,
  "rolesBeforeLlm": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1,
  "idempotentRoles": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ]
}
```

Exact-head validation after the lint/type follow-up:

```text
$ pnpm lint --threads=8
# passed

$ CI=true pnpm check:test-types
# passed

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

The earlier `1f2e39b6d9` addendum in this PR body remains the focused behavioral validation for `includeFamily + offset`, embedded/Gateway family-history parity, and carryover tests; this head only changes test typing/property-access style.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds service-level cron validation that rejects recurring schedules with `deleteAfterRun: true` on create or invalid re-enable while preserving legacy rename, disable, and clear-flag recovery paths, plus focused service and Gateway tests.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +196. Total +255 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main accepts explicit recurring `deleteAfterRun` on create, while the run-result path only deletes successful `at` jobs.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to choose the cron contract and the contributor branch needs Gateway/UI compatibility fixes before merge; this is not a safe automatic repair-only closeout.

target.title: Reject delete-after-run recurring cron jobs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`deleteAfterRun` is a one-shot lifecycle flag, but cron accepted jobs that combined it with recurring schedules. That leaves the scheduler with conflicting semantics: a job can be marked for deletion after a run while also being scheduled to run repeatedly.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds service-level validation so `deleteAfterRun: true` is only accepted for `schedule.kind="at"` on new jobs and on updates that would newly create or re-enable the invalid combination.

The update path is intentionally compatibility-aware for already-persisted legacy jobs: an existing recurring `deleteAfterRun` job can still be renamed, disabled, or cleared with `deleteAfterRun: false`. A disabled legacy-invalid job cannot be re-enabled until the flag is cleared or the schedule is changed to `at`.

## User Impact

Valid recurring jobs are unchanged. One-shot jobs still default to `deleteAfterRun`. Invalid new recurring delete-after-run jobs fail fast with a clear validation error instead of being persisted as scheduler state.

Users who already have an invalid persisted recurring job are not trapped: they can disable it, rename it, or clear `deleteAfterRun`; re-enabling is allowed after the job is valid again.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts` — 71 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts` — 42 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts -t "delete-after-run"` — 3 passed, 18 skipped
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check src/cron/service/jobs.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts`
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint src/cron/service/jobs.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Redacted live Gateway handler proof from a temp state directory against this branch:

```text
gateway add recurring deleteAfterRun: error cron deleteAfterRun is only supported for schedule.kind="at"
gateway rename legacy invalid: ok {"id":"legacy-delete-after-run-recurring","name":"renamed legacy invalid","enabled":true,"deleteAfterRun":true,"scheduleKind":"every"}
gateway disable legacy invalid: ok {"id":"legacy-delete-after-run-recurring","name":"renamed legacy invalid","enabled":false,"deleteAfterRun":true,"scheduleKind":"every"}
gateway reenable before clear: error invalid cron.update params: cron deleteAfterRun is only supported for schedule.kind="at"
gateway clear legacy flag: ok {"id":"legacy-delete-after-run-recurring","name":"renamed legacy invalid","enabled":false,"deleteAfterRun":false,"scheduleKind":"every"}
gateway reenable after clear: ok {"id":"legacy-delete-after-run-recurring","name":"renamed legacy invalid","enabled":true,"deleteAfterRun":false,"scheduleKind":"every"}
```

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

---

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

Supplemental (not a substitute for the live proof above), all in the same `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container at head `afe36ccf456f`: unit tests — **160 passed** across the gateway test projects (`tools-invoke-http` 42 + `tool-resolution` 5 + `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19, ×3 projects = 7 file-projects); `lint` (oxlint, 0/0), `tsgo:core` (0), `check:test-types` (0), and `plugin-sdk-surface-report` vitest (4/4). **`checks-node-core-fast` / plugin-SDK surface budget:** this PR adds `hostFsWrite?` as a *field* on the existing `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type — **zero new top-level exports** — so `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` passes on the `+1` budget already carried by #85664's base commit (`config-types` 416 / public 10285 / public-fn 5164 / deprecated 3238). No further budget change in this PR.

**Not tested:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` (RCE-class; out of scope for this PR and never exposed by the direct-invoke factory).
